hints.to adorn: That would be 'ornare' — 'Exoriare' comes from 'exoriri' (to arise)
hints.to pray: That would be 'orare' — 'Exoriare' comes from 'exoriri' (to arise)
hints.to begin: That would be 'ordiri' — 'Exoriare' comes from 'exoriri' (to arise)

steps.reveal: Exoriare = Arise! (2nd sg. imperative of deponent 'exoriri')

hints.present infinitive: While -are looks infinitive, 'exoriri' is deponent — 'exoriare' is the imperative singular
hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'exoriaris' — 'exoriare' is imperative of the deponent
hints.imperfect subjunctive: Imperfect subjunctive would be 'exorireris' — 'exoriare' is present imperative

hints.orior, oriri, ortus sum: These are the parts of 'oriri' (to rise) — the simple verb, not the compound
hints.orno, ornare, ornavi, ornatum: These are the parts of 'ornare' (to adorn/equip)
hints.oro, orare, oravi, oratum: These are the parts of 'orare' (to pray/speak)

steps.reveal: aliquis = someone (nom. sg., subject — the future avenger)

hints.ablative: Ablative would be 'aliquo' — 'aliquis' is nominative singular

hints.dative: While 'nostris' could be dative, the preposition 'ex' governs the ablative
hints.nominative: Nominative plural would be 'nostri' — 'nostris' is ablative with 'ex'
hints.genitive: Genitive plural would be 'nostrorum' — 'nostris' is ablative, governed by 'ex'

hints.dative: While 'ossibus' could be dative, 'ex' governs the ablative — 'from our bones'
hints.nominative: Nominative plural would be 'ossa' — 'ossibus' is ablative plural
hints.genitive: Genitive plural would be 'ossium' — 'ossibus' is ablative with 'ex'

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'ossis' ends in -is (3rd declension)
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'ossis' ends in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'ossis' ends in -is

steps.reveal: ultor = an avenger (nom. sg., in apposition with 'aliquis')

hints.vocative: Vocative would be 'ultor' too, but here it is nominative, in apposition with 'aliquis'

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'ultoris' ends in -is (3rd declension)
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'ultoris' ends in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'ultoris' ends in -is

translation: Arise, some avenger, from our bones
